The pattern suggests that in some cases, Catholic organizations saw Pacific nations as a “dumping ground” for abusive clergy. Pete McKenzie, a freelance journalist, explains.

2024-09-24.abstract: The earthquake occurred off the coast of Torishima Island, which is uninhabited, at 8:14 a.m. on Tuesday, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency. The advisory was lifted at 11 a.m.

2024-09-24.title: Thousands of Lebanese Are Displaced Amid Israeli Strikes
2024-09-24.abstract: Amal Mohamed Zayyat, a resident of southern Lebanon, sought shelter in Beirut after Israel stepped up its attacks against what it said were Hezbollah targets.

2024-09-24.title: In Lebanon’s Capital, Israel’s Strikes Stoke Fears of a Full-On War
2024-09-24.abstract: With Monday’s attacks across southern Lebanon, the prospect of the escalating conflict enveloping the city has suddenly become real for many Beirut residents.

2024-09-24.title: Ukrainian Poet and Rock Star Fights Near Front and Performs Behind It
2024-09-24.abstract: Serhiy Zhadan, 50, is a beloved Ukrainian poet as well as a novelist, lyricist and rock star. Furious over the invasion, he enlisted to fight even as his band still plays and his readings fill halls.

2024-09-24.title: How a Leftist Delivered Sri Lanka’s Biggest Political Jolt in Decades
2024-09-24.abstract: Anura Kumara Dissanayake offered his broad leftist coalition as the best hope for a different political culture, one not ruled by nepotism and corruption.

2024-09-25.title: A Litmus Test in Japan: Should Spouses Be Able to Have Different Surnames?
2024-09-25.abstract: The nine candidates vying to become prime minister have been differentiated by their stances on a law requiring married couples to share a family name.

2024-09-25.title: Taiwan and U.S. Work to Counter China’s Drone Dominance
2024-09-25.abstract: Officials hope that Taiwan can become a bigger player in the supply chain for drones, a move that would also help blunt any threats posed to the island by China.

2024-09-25.title: Israel Says It Shot Down Missile Over Tel Aviv
2024-09-25.abstract: The missile fired from Lebanon was the first time Hezbollah had launched an attack aimed at Tel Aviv, the military said.

2024-09-25.title: China Says It Launched a Rocket With a Dummy Warhead Into the Pacific
2024-09-25.abstract: The ballistic missile launch is a sign of the commitment of Xi Jinping, China’s leader, to continue strengthening the country’s Rocket Force and to project power in Asia.

2024-09-25.title: How Canadians Fell Out of Love With Justin Trudeau
2024-09-25.abstract: Canada’s Liberals are reeling from a string of setbacks, even as the party survived a no-confidence vote in Parliament on Wednesday.

2024-09-26.title: Mexican Senate Votes to Give Military Control of Civilian National Guard
2024-09-26.abstract: Mexico’s governing party says the guard would be more effective and less corrupt as part of the armed forces. Critics fear the increased militarization of civilian life and a spike in rights abuses.

2024-09-26.title: First It Was Lynch Mobs. Now Police Kill Pakistanis Accused of Blasphemy.
2024-09-26.abstract: The deaths of two men have reverberated across Pakistan, where the charge of insulting Islam has long been a sensitive issue.

2024-09-26.title: 200 Clashes a Day as Russia Races to Break Ukrainian Strongholds
2024-09-26.abstract: With fall approaching, Ukraine says its forces in the eastern Donetsk region are facing battle more frequently than they have in months.

2024-09-26.title: U.S. House Speaker Demands That Ukraine Fire Ambassador
2024-09-26.abstract: The public call from Speaker Mike Johnson deals a blow to Kyiv’s efforts to avoid the partisan acrimony of the U.S. presidential campaign.

2024-09-26.title: Israel’s Displaced Want to Go Home. But Will Attacks on Hezbollah Offer That Path?
2024-09-26.abstract: Israel said the goal of its strikes on Hezbollah was to allow residents of the north to go home. Some are hopeful that plan will work, but others worry that war may not bring safety.

2024-09-27.title: What’s at Stake in Austria’s General Election
2024-09-27.abstract: Austrians will head to the polls on Sunday to elect a new Parliament, with the Freedom Party in position to potentially form a far-right government for the first time since World War II.

2024-09-27.title: This Is What Can Land You in Jail for Sedition in Hong Kong
2024-09-27.abstract: Three men were the first to be convicted under the city’s recently expanded national security law, which has greatly curtailed political speech.

2024-09-27.title: Myanmar’s Military Asks Rebels to Stop Fighting and Join in Elections
2024-09-27.abstract: Though the offer was quickly rejected by several commanders of the opposition forces, it was still seen as a significant development in the three-year civil war.

2024-09-27.title: A ‘Super Bowl’ Holiday
2024-09-27.abstract: In the state of Victoria, you need more than one day to take in all that the A.F.L. Grand Final has to offer.

2024-09-27.title: Japan’s Governing Party Selects New Prime Minister
2024-09-27.abstract: Shigeru Ishiba, a former defense minister known for his plainspoken opinions, will replace Fumio Kishida next week.

2024-09-28.title: Israel Bombs Residential Site in Effort to Kill Hezbollah Leader
2024-09-28.abstract: The strike came barely an hour after the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, delivered a fiery address to the U.N. General Assembly.

2024-09-28.title: Israel’s Military Issues More Evacuation Warnings in Southern Beirut
2024-09-28.abstract: The military struck targets throughout the night that it said were tied to Hezbollah.

2024-09-28.title: Laborer’s Death Brings to Light Italy’s Conflicted Relationship With Migrants
2024-09-28.abstract: Italy, an aging country, badly needs migrant labor and immigration, but the government has admitted that the pathways for legal entry are rife with abuse.

2024-09-28.title: The American Drug Mules Smuggling Fentanyl Into the U.S.
2024-09-28.abstract: Mexican drug cartels are turning thousands of Americans into fentanyl smugglers, sending an army of couriers who can easily cross between both countries.

2024-09-28.title: Using Dance to Provoke, Delight and Tell South Africa’s Stories
2024-09-28.abstract: Growing up in a Black township, Vusi Mdoyi found a sprinkle of joy under apartheid in a street-dancing style known as pantsula. As a choreographer, he has elevated it into high art, injected with ideas.

2024-09-29.title: As Austrians Vote, Far Right Awaits Its Biggest Success
2024-09-29.abstract: The Freedom Party has made itself the country’s most popular party, with calls to bar asylum seekers. It is poised to come out on top in parliamentary elections for the first time.

2024-09-29.title: Iran’s Leaders Are Vulnerable, Divided and at a Crossroad on Response to Israel
2024-09-29.abstract: Leaders in Tehran suggested it would be the Lebanese militia that would strike back at Israel after the killing of Hassan Nasrallah and bombings in Beirut.

2024-09-29.title: Studying at an English-Speaking University? In Quebec, That May Cost Extra.
2024-09-29.abstract: Quebec says a new policy to charge some students higher tuition at top universities in Montreal is needed to preserve the province’s French identity.

2024-09-29.title: Why the World’s Biggest Powers Can’t Stop a Middle East War
2024-09-29.abstract: The United States’ ability to influence events in the Mideast has waned, and other major nations have essentially been onlookers.

2024-09-29.title: Netanyahu, Ignoring Allies and Defying Critics, Basks in a Rare Triumph
2024-09-29.abstract: Israel’s strike on Hassan Nasrallah was the culmination of several startling moves that suggest the Israeli prime minister feels unconstrained by foreign criticism.

2024-09-30.title: Beirut Building Damaged in Strike
2024-09-30.abstract: Footage showed emergency personnel responding to the strike on the Cola neighborhood of Beirut, Lebanon.

2024-09-30.title: A Blast in Central Beirut Damages a Building
2024-09-30.abstract: If the explosion is confirmed to be an Israeli attack, it would be the first Israeli strike within the Lebanese capital since the 2006 war with Hezbollah.

2024-09-30.title: A 180-Year-Old Jewish Paper Is Roiled by Fabrications and a Secret: Who Owns It?
2024-09-30.abstract: When several journalists resigned this month from the British newspaper over false news articles, they also raised a broader question about its ownership.

2024-09-30.title: How Two Soul Mates, Separated by War, Found Each Other Again
2024-09-30.abstract: Sofia Malina and Polina Muzhychkova met in the Ukrainian city of Mariupol, but were separated after it came under brutal assault by Russian forces. They were reunited after a long and harrowing saga.

2024-09-30.title: As Mexico’s President Steps Down, Some Laud His Legacy, but Others Fear It
2024-09-30.abstract: President Andrés Manuel López Obrador lifted millions out of poverty while eroding democratic norms, allowing his nationalist political movement to expand its sway.
